summary: Live-applied ADR 0276 from the latest rebased origin/main lineage by bringing the private NATS JetStream runtime and its preserved multi-principal auth catalog under repo-managed automation, recovering lv3-nats-jetstream after the latest server inspection found it exited during shared docker-runtime churn, reconciling PLATFORM_EVENTS, RAG_DOCUMENT, and SECRET_ROTATION to no drift, and re-verifying platform, secret-rotation, and RAG publishes end to end.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest -m pytest -q tests/test_nats_jetstream_runtime_role.py tests/test_secret_rotation.py tests/test_service_id_resolver.py tests/unit/test_event_taxonomy.py tests/unit/test_ledger_writer.py tests/test_ansible_execution_scopes.py` returned `38 passed in 0.68s` on the rebased latest-main branch.

`uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/ansible_scope_runner.py run --inventory inventory/hosts.yml --run-id ws0276syntax-postrebase --playbook playbooks/nats-jetstream.yml --env production -- --private-key /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump --syntax-check` passed, and `make preflight WORKFLOW=live-apply-service` also passed on the synchronized worktree.

`ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING=False uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/ansible_scope_runner.py run --inventory inventory/hosts.yml --run-id ws0276recover-r1 --playbook playbooks/nats-jetstream.yml --env production -- --private-key /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump` succeeded with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=79 changed=1 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=6 rescued=0 ignored=0` after the latest server check found `lv3-nats-jetstream` had exited.

The refreshed guest-local status capture kept `lv3-nats-jetstream` `Up`, `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8222/healthz` returned `{"status":"ok"}`, and the JetStream monitor listed the expected `PLATFORM_EVENTS`, `RAG_DOCUMENT`, and `SECRET_ROTATION` streams alongside the preserved KV and workflow/alert/receipt/agent streams.

`make check-nats-streams` and `make apply-nats-streams` both returned `PLATFORM_EVENTS: none`, `RAG_DOCUMENT: none`, and `SECRET_ROTATION: none`, proving the controller-side stream contract now sees no drift after the replay.

verification.check: Platform, secret-rotation, and RAG smoke publishes
verification.observed: `uv run --with pyyaml --with nats-py python scripts/nats_streams.py --smoke-publish` published `platform.findings.observation` into `PLATFORM_EVENTS` with `seq=169`, and follow-up raw JSON publishes to `secret.rotation.completed` and `rag.document.staged` reached `SECRET_ROTATION` and `RAG_DOCUMENT` with stream-local sequence `2` and `stream_message_count=2`.

notes: This receipt records the rebased branch-local latest-main proof before the protected release, README, and canonical platform-truth surfaces are updated.

The earlier pre-rebase ws-0276 evidence remains in the branch for audit history, but this receipt supersedes it after origin/main advanced to release 0.177.96 and the synchronized branch replay now points at a surviving commit in the current lineage.

Latest server inspection found `lv3-nats-jetstream` exited even though the earlier branch-local replay had passed; rerunning the governed playbook recovered the runtime and restored green controller-side stream automation.

VERSION, changelog release surfaces, docs/release-notes, versions/stack.yaml platform_version, build/platform-manifest.json, the integrated README summary, ADR 0276 metadata, and docs/adr/.index.yaml still wait for the exact-main integration step.

correction_loop.repair_action: Reran playbooks/nats-jetstream.yml from rebased commit 959b10f382ff96e7a16db56a1283e83cca9fb8ea after latest server inspection found lv3-nats-jetstream exited.
correction_loop.verification: NATS health returned ok, make check-nats-streams and make apply-nats-streams both reported no drift, and platform plus cross-domain smoke publishes landed in the expected streams.
